reviews.review_msg: Open-book quizzes with asynchronous lectures. Really organized with Canvas and email. Content can be tough to grasp but Betty teaches us to think critically. I recommend taking her class after youve taken a few SFU English courses beforehand. As the reviewer below said, she will not hold your hand but she will do her best to help you succeed.

The assignments were pretty straightforward and Betty offers examples of what she wants. She also gave us flexibility with our evaluations which was nice. She is really helpful in office hours and answers emails really fast. I really enjoyed this class and I would recommend taking it if you actually want to learn how to read and write.

Betty is very knowledgeable and well respected, but she seems to have difficulty translating this into engaging lectures, and she is an incredibly nit-picky and particular marker. Also, she is not very understanding of students' extenuating circumstances as she refuses to budge on due dates (even in the middle of a pandemic).

Betty is a very kind, respected prof and I quite enjoy our synchronous lectures (taking it online). That being said, she gives difficult assignments with very little instructions and grades tough. I would recommend her but be prepared to work really hard on all your papers for only an okay grade.

Betty truly cares about teaching her students. She puts time and thought into every class. That being said, she expects you to stay on top of the readings and engage with the course content--but her passion for her field is contagious. Betty's course was more than worth my time and money and she is a quality educator. Highly recommended!

If you like to be bored, take this class. The MOST useless topics are included. Her lectures are full of nonsense and random things that fall out of topic. I understand english may be mundane BUT DO NOT take a class with her if you hate being off topic all the time. Example: 50 min lecture, 10 mins of useful notes

I would say your marks are all depended on your TA. Here I just want to address Maya,my best TA this term, always provides help and is super nice. Betty lectures well, but her stressful 10 minutes in class writings are not fair. Especially her super tough marking scheme.

Your mark depends on your TA, lectures are just good to go to for in class writings worth marks. Had Rawia as my TA & she was absolutely amazing I couldn't believe it was her first time TA'ing! She had great office hours, was very helpful, genuinely cared, and was a fair marker. She prepared us well for each essay & is super approachable !

90% of lectures were useless, I showed up only bc there are random in class writings for marks which is ridiculous bc it only tests you ability to write fast and under pressure in less than 10 minutes?? Your mark is based off 2 essays, midterm (worth 5%), final, tutorial mark, and than small things like preparation etc worth 10%.
Very enthusiastic about content, and a lot of grading depends on lecture material.
